hardware.wifi: none (5G/NR modem only, no WiFi radio)

modem.note: AT commands via /dev/ttyUSB2 at 115200 baud. uqmi via /dev/cdc-wdm0. Modem firmware queryable via AT+GMR or sysfs /sys/bus/usb/devices/2-1/product.

hardware.serial: UART, 57600 8N1, internal header J5 (Pin1=GND, Pin2=key/empty, Pin3=RX, Pin4=TX, Pin5=3.3V). Source: OpenWrt patch by Bjørn Mork (2021-06). Note: NOT 115200 — confirmed from OpenWrt device tree.
hardware.note: No WiFi — 5G modem only. Single PoE ethernet port.

zycast.description: Flash via ZyXEL multicast protocol (zycast). Flashes both Kernel and Kernel2 partitions simultaneously. Uses initramfs-recovery image with zytrx header. No password required. TESTED on hardware (2026-05-26, Go binary on MIPS OpenWrt switch, NR7101 Telenor).
zycast.reset_instructions: Power cycle the device. Z-Loader enters Multiboot Listening automatically on EVERY boot for ~7 seconds (1s ESC prompt + 6s countdown). Serial interrupt NOT required — just start zycast before power cycle and the bootloader picks it up. Serial-verified 2026-06-27.
zycast.led_pattern: no specific LED — watch console or multicast traffic
zycast.zycast_binary: conwrt scripts/zycast.py (C binary with Python fallback)

sysupgrade.description: SSH sysupgrade from existing OpenWrt. SCP image to /tmp/ then run sysupgrade -n. Use scp -O (legacy protocol) as OpenWrt dropbear may lack SFTP. Boot takes ~60s after flash.
sysupgrade.scp_note: Use scp -O flag — OpenWrt dropbear lacks /usr/libexec/sftp-server

stock_firmware.source: zytrx header format from zytrx.c (openwrt/firmware-utils), bmork mailing list posts
stock_firmware.http_redirect: NOT LuCI — stock firmware uses Zyxel proprietary web UI

openwrt_running.no_dhcp_server: does NOT run DHCP server by default on LAN (unlike most routers)
openwrt_running.no_zycast_multicast: does NOT send multicast to 225.0.0.0:5631 during normal boot

zycast_listening.source: serial-verified on hardware 2026-06-27, also bmork forum post + zycast.c source
zycast_listening.boot_sequence: DDR3 cal → U-Boot 1.1.3 → Ralink UBoot 5.0.0.0 → Z-Loader V1.30 → 'Hit ESC key' (1s) → 'Multiboot Listening...' (6s countdown) → firmware handoff
zycast_listening.note: SERIAL-VERIFIED: Z-Loader enters Multiboot Listening on EVERY boot automatically for ~7 seconds. Serial interrupt NOT required. Just start zycast before power cycle.

openwrt_booting.no_dhcp_offer: no DHCP server running on LAN by default
openwrt_booting.icmp_unreachable: may send ICMP net unreachable for WAN traffic (e.g. ZeroTier)

partition_layout.source: verified on hardware 2026-05-19 and 2026-06-28 (cat /proc/mtd via serial)

mtd2.name: Factory
mtd2.note: Serial, certs, calibration — MUST backup before zycast

mtd5.name: Kernel2
mtd5.note: Backup/recovery firmware partition — ALSO overwritten by zycast

partition_layout.note: Dual-partition: Kernel (primary) + Kernel2 (recovery). Bootloader BootingFlag selects partition. zycast ATNR writes both simultaneously.

backup.note: SSH as root/supervisor with generated password. Dump all MTD partitions before zycast. Factory partition is irreplaceable.

modem_inventory.mode: QMI (AT+QCFG=usbnet,0). Confirmed: qmi_wwan driver on interface 1.4, class ff/ff/ff
modem_inventory.note: AT commands work via raw echo+cat to /dev/ttyUSB2 (no picocom/stty needed on OpenWrt). uqmi fails after sysupgrade -n because modem interface is unconfigured. AT commands always work.

uqmi_commands.note: Only works after modem WAN interface is configured (uci set network.wan.proto=qmi). Returns 'Failed to connect to service' on fresh sysupgrade -n.

sysupgrade.verified_with: sysupgrade -n 24.10.5→25.12.4, SSH key auth after ~60s boot
sysupgrade.notes: sysupgrade -n from 24.10.5 to 25.12.4. SCP required -O flag (legacy SCP protocol) because dropbear lacks SFTP server. Boot took ~60s. SSH key auth worked immediately after boot. LuCI available on port 80.

zycast.verified_with: Go zycast binary on MIPS OpenWrt switch, NR7101 Telenor (MAC 78:c5:7d:13:91:9c), PoE power cycle, initramfs-recovery image
zycast.notes: Flashed via Go zycast binary cross-compiled for MIPS. Sent on switch.1002 (VLAN 1002, lan2 untagged) to multicast 225.0.0.0:5631. PoE power cycle with 7-10s off delay. Device boots OpenWrt at 192.168.1.1 after ~20s. Header format verified byte-for-byte against C zycast.c reference. Overwrites BOTH Kernel and Kernel2 partitions — stock firmware gone. IMPORTANT: monitor BOTH the old stock IP (192.168.2.1) and the OpenWrt IP (192.168.1.1) after flashing — the IP changes and if 192.168.1.0/24 conflicts with your local network, you must SSH through the switch.

modem_inventory.verified_with: AT commands on /dev/ttyUSB2: ATI, AT+GMR, AT+QCCID all responded
modem_inventory.notes: Quectel RG502Q-EA identified via sysfs (vendor=2c7c, product=0800). AT commands on /dev/ttyUSB2 work reliably for model, firmware, SIM status, ICCID. Firmware: RG502QEAACR13A03M4G_ZYXEL (Zyxel-custom). SIM present. Modem in QMI mode (qmi_wwan driver). uqmi returns 'Failed to connect to service' after sysupgrade -n (modem interface unconfigured). AT commands always work regardless of network config.

cellular_data.verified_with: SIM card (ice+), ping 8.8.8.8 via LTE, 56Mbps down, 43ms latency
cellular_data.notes: uci set network.wan.apn=ice.net && uqmi ifup wan. OpenWrt ships with QMI WAN pre-configured (proto=qmi, device=/dev/cdc-wdm0). Only APN change needed. Modem registers as home (not roaming). DNS: XXX.XXX.XXX.X, XXX.XXX.XXX.X. Connection comes up in ~15s after ifup wan.

default_wan_config.note: OpenWrt 25.12.4 ships with QMI WAN pre-configured. Only APN needs to be set per carrier.

setup_commands.note: Wait ~15s after ifup for connection. uqmi returns 'connected' when data session is active.

serial_configuration.method: Serial console after OpenWrt initramfs boot
operations: uci set network.lan.ipaddr — change LAN IP via serial
uci set dropbear PasswordAuth — enable/disable password SSH
serial_configuration.note: Serial console provides full root shell after OpenWrt boot. Essential when device IP conflicts with another router on the network. Use scripts/serial-configure.py for structured configuration.

stages.name: procd init
stages.serial_output: Please press Enter to activate this console

boot_sequence.pcie_note: PCIe init (Quectel modem) is timing-sensitive — may hang on some boots. If kernel hangs at mt7621-pci, power cycle and retry. PCIe init succeeds on subsequent attempts.
boot_sequence.uart_break_note: FT232R serial adapter gets stuck after UART break during power cycle. Use serial-boot-capture.py with --recovery-wait to auto-recover.
